title: Human Reports, Machine Output, Terminal Width, and Accessibility · Program Brief: No Color Only
summary: The report encodes meaning only with color, clips long words, changes columns by terminal width, mixes diagnostics with JSON stdout, and gives screen-reader users an unlabeled number grid. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the no color only increment in accessible human and machine report system. Learners prog…
objectives: Keep every status and distinction understandable without color and honor plain or no-color operation.
Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-no-color-only — Keep every status and distinction understandable without color and honor plain or no-color operation.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-report-format-accessibility-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-report-format-accessibility-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_report_format_accessibility_4(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-no-color-only-1l2akx
# Competency: bookbot-no-color-only.
# Case 6294: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_no_color_only_1l2akx(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1l2akx = "1l2akx-xka2l1-6294"
    assert evidence_variant_1l2akx
    evidence_axes_1l2akx = (
        "corpus1l2akx",
        "policyxka2l1",
        "revision6294",
        "command1l2akx6294",
        "reportxka2l16294",
        "test62941l2akx",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1l2akx)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep every status and distinction understandable without color and honor plain or no-color operation.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the No Color Only model · Theory No Color Only, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, No Color Only precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the No Color Only model · Theory No Color Only, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 3230 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep every status and distinction understandable without color and honor plain or no-color operation.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the No Color Only model · Theory No Color Only, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: ANSI color improves accessibility even when it is the only error signal.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-no-color-only-1l2akx.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-no-color-only-1l2akx(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_no_color_only_1l2akx\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?report_rows < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not diagnostics_on_stderr)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?color_only_signals > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not structured_alternative)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
